raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a XXXX XXXX from the XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year>. When I authorized the charge on the app, I applied a promo code that reduced the original amount from {$130.00} to XXXX for a {$13.00} discount. This item does not have a return option. When I received my email receipt, it was {$13.00} higher. I attempted to resolve the issue with the XXXX, and they offered me a {$13.00} discount on a future purchase. I rejected the offer. 

I filed a dispute with my credit card company on the website and in writing. I explained it was a fraudulent charge and provided emails between me and the vendor proving they knew of the problem with the promo code and only offered a discount and not return the money they stole from me. 

The credit card company denied my chargeback request because I had received the item. I again explained why that {$13.00} was a fraud, and they only said they could reopen the case. I'm hoping you can force them to review it again.
